overview.whyItMatters: GenAI applications process sensitive data: customer information, business secrets, regulated content. Data security and privacy are non-negotiable for enterprise deployment. Understanding how to protect data throughout the GenAI pipeline is essential.

concepts.title: Network Isolation and Secure Access
concepts.content:
Production GenAI workloads should not traverse the public internet. VPC endpoints provide private connectivity to AWS services.

**VPC Endpoints for Bedrock:**
Create interface endpoints for Bedrock in your VPC. Traffic between your compute and Bedrock stays within AWS network. No internet gateway required. Endpoint policies control which principals can use the endpoint and which actions they can perform.

**VPC Endpoints for SageMaker:**
Similar setup for SageMaker endpoints. Private connectivity to model inference. Endpoint policies enforce access controls.

**IAM Data Access Policies:**
IAM policies control who can access what data. Condition keys enable fine-grained control:
- Source VPC/VPC endpoint restrictions
- Resource tagging requirements
- Time-based access windows
- MFA requirements for sensitive operations

**Lake Formation:**
Centralized data governance for data lakes. Define fine-grained access controls (column-level, row-level). Grant GenAI workloads appropriate data access without over-permissioning. Audit all data access.

**CloudWatch Monitoring:**
Monitor for unusual access patterns. Set alarms for unexpected data access volumes, new principals accessing sensitive data, or access from unusual locations.
keyPoints: VPC endpoints keep traffic off public internet
Endpoint policies control access at network level
IAM policies with conditions for fine-grained control
Lake Formation for data lake governance
concepts.examTip: 'Private connectivity' or 'no internet' -> VPC endpoints for Bedrock/SageMaker.

concepts.title: PII Detection and Protection
concepts.content:
Personally identifiable information (PII) requires special handling. Detect it, protect it, and minimize its exposure.

**Amazon Comprehend PII Detection:**
Detect PII in text before it reaches models. Identify: names, addresses, SSN, credit cards, phone numbers, emails, dates of birth, and more. Use for pre-processing to flag or redact PII.

**Amazon Macie for S3:**
Discover and classify sensitive data in S3 buckets. Macie scans your data stores, identifies PII and other sensitive content, and creates findings. Know where your sensitive data lives before feeding it to GenAI.

**Bedrock Native Privacy:**
Bedrock has built-in privacy protections:
- Your data is NOT used to train foundation models
- Data encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest
- Data isolated per customer
- Audit logging of all API calls

**Guardrails PII Filtering:**
Bedrock Guardrails can detect and handle PII in inputs and outputs:
- Block: Reject content containing PII
- Mask: Replace PII with placeholders
- Configure which PII types to handle

**S3 Lifecycle Policies:**
Automate data retention. Old data with PII should be deleted according to retention policies. S3 Lifecycle rules automate transitions (to cheaper storage) and deletions.
keyPoints: Comprehend: Detect PII in text before model processing
Macie: Discover sensitive data in S3 data stores
Bedrock: Data NOT used for training, encrypted, isolated
Guardrails: Block or mask PII in inputs/outputs

concepts.title: Data Anonymization Strategies
concepts.content:
Sometimes you need to process data without exposing identifying information. Anonymization removes or obscures PII while preserving analytical value.

**Data Masking:**
Replace PII with fake but realistic values. Original: 'John Smith, 555-123-4567'. Masked: 'Alice Brown, 555-987-6543'. Preserves format and relationships without real data.

**Tokenization:**
Replace sensitive values with tokens. Store mapping separately and securely. Original processing uses tokens; real values retrieved only when needed.

**Redaction:**
Remove PII entirely. Original: 'Contact John at john@email.com'. Redacted: 'Contact [NAME] at [EMAIL]'. Simple but may lose context.

**Differential Privacy:**
Add noise to aggregate queries so individual records can't be identified. Useful for analytics on sensitive datasets.

**Comprehend for Detection:**
Comprehend identifies PII types and locations. Use this to target masking/redaction precisely. Detect before anonymizing.

**When to Anonymize:**
- Before storing in training datasets
- Before sharing with third parties
- Before logging/monitoring systems
- Before less-privileged systems access data
keyPoints: Masking: Replace PII with fake realistic values
Tokenization: Replace with tokens, store mapping securely
Redaction: Remove PII entirely with placeholders
Anonymize before training data, logging, sharing

skillExplanations.explanation:
Secure environments isolate GenAI workloads from public internet. VPC endpoints create private connections to Bedrock and SageMaker. No traffic traverses internet gateways.

IAM policies control data access. Use conditions for fine-grained control: restrict to specific VPCs, require specific tags, enforce MFA. Least privilege: grant only necessary permissions.

Lake Formation centralizes data governance. Define who can access what data at column and row level. GenAI workloads get appropriate access without blanket permissions. All access is audited.

CloudWatch monitoring detects anomalies. Unusual access patterns, new principals, unexpected volumes. Alarms notify security teams of potential issues.

awsServices.whenToUse: VPC endpoints for private Bedrock/SageMaker connectivity.
Access policies with conditions for data access control.
Fine-grained data lake access control and governance.
Monitor for unusual access patterns and anomalies.

skillExplanations.explanation:
PII protection starts with detection. Comprehend identifies PII types in text: names, addresses, SSN, etc. Use before model processing to flag content requiring special handling.

Macie scans S3 data stores. Discover where PII exists in your data. Create remediation plans for unexpected PII. Monitor for new sensitive data.

Bedrock has built-in privacy: your data isn't used for model training, encryption protects data at rest and in transit, customer data is isolated. Guardrails add runtime PII filtering—block or mask PII in inputs and outputs.

S3 Lifecycle policies automate retention. Data should be deleted according to policy. Automate transitions and deletions rather than relying on manual processes.

awsServices.whenToUse: Detect PII in text before or after model processing.
Discover and classify sensitive data in S3.
Runtime PII filtering in inputs and outputs.
Lifecycle policies for automated data retention.

skillExplanations.explanation:
Anonymization removes identifying information while preserving data utility. Data masking replaces real values with fake but realistic ones. Format and relationships preserved.

Comprehend detects PII for targeted anonymization. Don't anonymize blindly; identify what needs protection and handle it appropriately. Different PII types may need different treatment.

Anonymization strategies include masking (fake values), tokenization (reversible mapping), redaction (removal), and differential privacy (noise addition). Choose based on use case: is reversibility needed? Is format important? How sensitive is the data?

examStrategies.approach: VPC endpoints for Bedrock/SageMaker. No internet gateway needed. Endpoint policies for additional control.
Amazon Macie for S3 data classification. Comprehend for text content analysis.
Bedrock Guardrails PII filters. Configure to block or mask. Apply to both inputs and outputs.

keyTakeaways: VPC endpoints provide private connectivity to Bedrock/SageMaker
Comprehend detects PII in text; Macie discovers PII in S3
Bedrock: data NOT used for training, encrypted, isolated
Guardrails PII filters: block or mask PII at runtime
Anonymization: masking, tokenization, redaction based on needs
commonMistakes: Running GenAI workloads over public internet instead of VPC endpoints
Not knowing where PII exists before starting GenAI projects
Forgetting Bedrock's built-in privacy guarantees (data not used for training)
Applying same anonymization to all PII regardless of sensitivity
No data retention policies for GenAI logs and outputs
